A cloak that manifested from an especially forbidden page from the University
Library through unknown means. It now seeks purpose.
dropLocation: Fire Over Morgrave, end chest

buffs.type: Power of the Flames of Purity

name: Page Regalia: Exiled Tactica
description:

dropLocation: Fire Over Morgrave, end chest

name: Page Regalia: Unsanctioned Arcana
description:
This page, filled with knowledge and power from other tomes across Eberron,
gained so much power it somehow manifested into a Cloak. It now seeks purpose.
dropLocation: Fire Over Morgrave, end chest

name: Phasecloak
description:
This cloak is emblazoned with an indecipherable symbol. Examining it more closely,
it looks like the symbol is written in blood...
dropLocation: A Raven at the Door, End chest, Rescue Yesper From The Dungeons optional chest

name: Phiarlan Mirror Cloak
description:
House Phiarlan spies keep many tools to help them in their trade. This cloak
is a prime example of such a tool, and reflects light in such a way that the
wearer is made partially invisible. This effect also wards it against certain
harmful spells.
dropLocation: Partycrashers, Rest Room chest; End chest

name: Platinum Mirror Cape
description:
This platinum coated cape is ostentatious and incredible. It's probably worth
quite a bit, but it'd be a shame to sell something so unique.
dropLocation: Another Man's Treasure, End chest

name: Quilted Cape
description:
A mother-of-pearl clasps fastens this patchwork garment across its wearer's
shoulders. It seems to dull ambient sounds.
dropLocation: Cult of the Six, End reward

name: Razorclaw's Mantle
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: Advance to level 15, End reward

name: Resonant Cloth
description: Wearing this makes you feel just a bit more alive.
dropLocation: Total Chaos, End Chest

name: Ruined Glory
description:
This tattered cloth was once a shining gold before being lost to the sewers,
but its defensive magic still lingers in it.
dropLocation: Kill Ten Rats, End Chest

name: Sacrarium Cloak
description:
The Auricular Sacrarium is a cabal of evil Kopru that have found refuge on the
Isle of Dread.
dropLocation: Isle of Dread (wilderness), rare encounter chest

name: Sage's Mantle (Level 19)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: The Tracker's Trap, End reward

name: Sage's Mantle (Level 27)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: The Tracker's Trap, End reward

name: Sailcloth Cloak
description: This cloak appears to have been fashioned from the sail of a seaworthy vessel.
dropLocation: Under the Cover of Darkness, end chest

name: Sanguine Sash of Estelar
description: A remarkable sash, fit for a Dragon disguising themselves in humanoid form.
dropLocation: Burning Down the House, end chest (rare )

name: Scarlet Scale Cloak
description:
Made to resemble dragonscale, this cloak isn't much protection versus an open
flame. But nothing looks better on a Dragonborn.
dropLocation: The Final Vintage, End chest

name: Scoundrel's Half-Cloak
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: Advance to level 15, End reward

name: Scurvy's Wrap
description: This magical cloak, a trophy of battle, is magically enhanced.
dropLocation: Irestone Inlet, Scurvy 's chest

name: Shadowhail Cloak
description:
When you wear this cloak, you are somehow able to see in the darkest of nights.
Your foes, however, cannot.
dropLocation: Sealed in Amber, End chest & optional draw Strahd's attention chest

name: Shimmer
description:
The magical threads woven into this cloak were designed to be attractive to
others. Even in pitch darkness, the cloak lures passersby like a siren's song.
dropLocation: Third Time's a Charm, End Chest

name: Silver Dragonscale Capelet
description: Shines with brilliant silver scales.
dropLocation: Too Hot to Handle, End Chest

name: Silverthread Cloak
description: This cloak was fashioned from thread made of pure silver.
dropLocation: Into the Mists, end chest

name: Skirmisher's Cloak (Level 19)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: The Tracker's Trap, End reward

name: Skirmisher's Cloak (Level 25)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: The Tracker's Trap, End reward

name: Slate-Scale Capelet
description:
This capelet is formed from a mosaic of tiny, scale-shaped pieces of slate.
The enchantments afforded to it offer a surprising amount of protection to
the wearer.
dropLocation: Records of the Past, End chest

name: Slave Master's Cloak
description:
Ripped from the back of a Slave Master, this cloak once instilled terror among
the ranks.
dropLocation: Secret of the Slavers' Stockade, End chest

name: Stormreaver's Napkin
description:
You hold in your hand the huge cloth napkin that was used by the Stormreaver
in his final meal before the great cataclysm. You can't tell if the red stains
on its surface are of wine or blood, but it does seem that everything is so
much easier to understand now.
dropLocation: The Reaver's Fate, Raid warded chest

name: Stormreaver's Tablecloth
description:
You hold in your hand a small shred of the huge tablecloth that was used by
the Stormreaver in his final meal before the great cataclysm. You can't tell
if the red stains on its surface are of wine or blood, but it does seem that
everything is so much easier to understand now.
dropLocation: The Fall of Truth, Raid warded chest (Epic Normal)

name: Stormsinger Cloak
description:
Some harpies of the Stormsinger flight wear cloaks such as this to increase
their musical prowess. It will also protect the wearer with violent sonic energy.
dropLocation: Assault on Summerfield, Captain Markus ' chest and optional chests

name: Symphony
description:
The scales along this cloak tap each other as it billows, and the faint sound
of music can be heard on the wind.
dropLocation: Riding the Storm Out, Warded chest

name: The Deathly Shroud
dropLocation: Relentless, end chest

name: The Invisible Cloak of Strahd
description:
This cloak is perfectly tailored and incredibly comfortable. Pity you can't
see it. It appears Strahd enchanted it to render itself invisible in the event
of his demise.
dropLocation: Old Baba's Hut, Raid Chest

name: Thin Black Cloak
description: This black cloak will increase your dexterity.
dropLocation: The Bugbear Bandits, End Chest, and Caged Beast, End chest

name: Toxic Sash of Drysvayla
description: A remarkable sash, fit for a Dragon disguising themselves in humanoid form.
dropLocation: Going Rogue, end chest (rare )

name: Trailblazer's Tattered Scarf
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: Advance to level 15, End reward

name: Twisthallow Cloak
description:
This tattered cloak was left behind in the wake of one trying to flee Barovia.
Unsuccessfully, one might add.
dropLocation: Mad Tea Party, End chest

name: Unicorn's Rememberance
dropLocation: Toil and Trouble, end chest

name: University Archer's Cloak
description:
Though it's a bit frayed from the winds and time, there's still possibility
weaved into this cloak's threads.
dropLocation: Enemy of My Enemy, End chest

name: University Defender's Cloak
description: This thick capelet has seen better days, but could likely be restored to glory.
dropLocation: Grand Theft Aureon, End chest

name: University Duelist's Cloak
description: Once used for a secretive dueling club, this cloak's future is now boundless.
dropLocation: Taken in Hand, End chest

name: University Researcher's Cloak
description:
Reseachers from the university are often seen with these cloaks, but something
feels as if it's missing from this one.
dropLocation: The Devils to Pay, End chest

name: Walking Ancestor's Shroud
description: This burial shroud befits one of the Isle of Dread's Walking Ancestors.
dropLocation: Whispers of Return, end chest

name: Widow Weave Cloak
description:
This cloak is a by-product of the Kar'Thul experimentation on Whisperdoom and
her spawn. The magically altered spiders were found to produce strong silk
imbued with special properties. Months of delicate work and treatment went
into this item's fabrication and few were actually produced. These cloaks were
likely reserved for Kar'Thul of high regard.
dropLocation: The Spawn of Whisperdoom, Turn in Silver Goblet to the Ungurz Lieutenant for a small chance to get it

name: Witchdoctor's Mantle
description:
A ceremonial mantle worm by hobgoblin witchdoctors, providing them with great
mystical insight.
dropLocation: The Seal of Shan-To-Kor, End reward

name: Wretched Twilight
description:
Nerezza's shadow is made of a material completely foreign to you; it flows with
your body yet feels like you are wearing nothing.
dropLocation: Ascension Chamber, Raid Warded chest
